Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Noctuoidea; Erebidae; Lymantriinae; Lymantria
Assembly: The assembly ilLymMona1.2 is based on 39x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Qiagen Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Viridiplantae; Streptophyta; Embryophyta; Bryophyta; Bryophytina; Bryopsida; Bryidae; Bryanae; Bryales; Mniaceae; Pohlia
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Viridiplantae; Streptophyta; Embryophyta; Bryophyta; Bryophytina; Bryopsida; Bryidae; Bryanae; Bryales; Mniaceae; Pohlia
Assembly: The assembly cbPohNuta1.1 is based on 104x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This assembly appears to suggest that this species is a degraded tetraploid with weak interacting HiC signal between putative homeologous chromosomes. Multiple inversions can be observed between the homeologs, and also large fusion/fission events. Chromosome 15 was split into two homeologs, chromosome 22 and 8. In addition chromosome 8 was fused to a new chromosome and there is also chromosome 21, which seems to not have a homeolog.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Tineoidea; Gracillariidae; Phyllonorycter
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Tineoidea; Gracillariidae; Phyllonorycter
Assembly: The assembly ilPhyMess1.hap1.1 is based on 40x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The sex chromosome was identified based on copy number on the diploid HIC map. We identified two potential B chromosomes and both are in HAP1 file. We believe they are B chromosomes because: i) after BUSCO alignment to a close related species, all Phyllonorycter messaniella autosomes match one of the reference chromosomes, looking like that most of the content present in B chromosomes may be new to the genome; ii) the only two BUSCO hits present in the B chromosomes were duplicated and iii) similarity could be observed on the HiC map between the B chromosomes and some tiny regions of some of the autosomes, indicating they may have genes in common.

The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Assembly: The assembly ilTilCitr1.1 is based on 96x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosomes Z and W were assigned by read coverage statistics.

Assembly: The assembly ilPedArid1.hap2.1 is based on 23x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. The order and orientation of scaffolds making up the W chromosomes is uncertain. Chromosomes 10 and 17 underwent fission/fusion event, and is represented as two separate chromosomes (chr10A, chr10B and chr17A, chr17B) on hap2. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 2 (4.1 - 5.7 Mbp) and on chromosome 20 (14.8 - 15.2 Mbp) are unknown.
